text.title: Decomposition: Django Monolith to Bounded Apps Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the decomposition-django methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: App boundaries follow ownership (one team owns one app), not technical layers (no 'models' app, no 'utils' app).
rule.rationale: Layer-based decomposition produces the same cross-imports back; ownership-based decomposition matches the deploy unit.

rule.statement: Each app exposes its public surface via a `contracts/` module (functions + types). Cross-app reaches outside `contracts/` are forbidden.
rule.rationale: A named public surface is the only way to enforce 'small public, large private' inside one repo.

rule.statement: An importlinter.ini contract enforces app dependency direction; CI fails on violation.
rule.rationale: Without machine enforcement, the contract decays in weeks.

rule.statement: When moving a model to a new app, do it in one PR: create new app, move file, write data migration with RunPython for FK rewrites, delete old reference.

rationale.source: Django docs; internal — Q4 2025 model-move audit.

rule.rationale: Multi-PR moves leave orphan ContentType rows and broken migrations; one PR keeps the schema coherent.

rule.statement: Each app owns its tasks module; tasks call services in the same app only. Cross-app effects go via an app's contracts function, not a direct task import.
rule.rationale: Cross-app task imports replicate the model-import tangle they were supposed to fix.

rule.statement: Model moves include a RunPython data migration that updates ContentType.app_label so generic relations keep working.
rule.rationale: Stale ContentType rows break GenericForeignKey and admin in production weeks after the move.

rule.statement: If the repo is < 5k LOC OR team is single-developer OR stack is being rewritten off Django soon, skip this methodology.
rule.rationale: Decomposition pays back at scale; below threshold it adds ceremony without benefit.
